compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Finding (SC1 — verified)
The mandatory completion-verification gate imports a PRIVATE sibling function across a module boundary with no public contract, so refactoring `operations` can silently break the gate.

Citations: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py:90-92` (`from rebar.llm import operations` then `operations._assemble_context(...)`); def private at `src/rebar/llm/operations.py:69`. (A prior identical leak — `completion._child_closure_findings` etc. — was fixed by promoting to public names; this one was missed.)

## Remediation
Promote `_assemble_context` to a documented public function (rename to `assemble_context` or add a thin public `build_gate_context` wrapper) with a stated return contract; the gate imports the public name.

cascade_flag: private->public rename — sweep other callers of `_assemble_context`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/llm/operations.py` exposes a public, docstring'd context builder
- [ ] `gate_ops.py` imports the public name (no leading-underscore cross-module import)
- [ ] Existing completion-gate behavior unchanged (tests green)
compiled_state.title: Give the completion gate a public context contract (promote operations._assemble_context)
